draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to approachable, friendly interfaces with gentle curves than sharp geometric precision
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Explore softer, more human-centered aesthetic direction for the CRM
facade.rejectImplies: Test cleaner, more minimalist or structured visual language next

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to approachable, friendly interfaces with gentle curves than sharp geometric precision
evidence.implication: Explore softer, more human-centered aesthetic direction for the CRM

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer conversational, approachable voice over corporate/formal language in their CRM tools
facade.label: Casual chatty tone
facade.acceptImplies: Build with warm, informal copy; use 'we' language; encourage personality in UI microcopy
facade.rejectImplies: Lean toward professional, neutral, business-standard voice; minimize personality; prioritize clarity over warmth

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer interfaces that feel personal and approachable rather than corporate or cold
facade.label: Soft, warm interface
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should use warm colors, soft shadows, rounded corners, and a personal tone in copy—feeling like a supportive tool rather than corporate software
facade.rejectImplies: The CRM should lean toward cool tones, clean geometric lines, and formal language—prioritizing clarity and efficiency over emotional comfort

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer snappy, click-triggered modals over animated slide-ins for quick client lookups
facade.axisTargeted: Animation style: instant appearance vs smooth transition
facade.label: Instant client pop-up
facade.acceptImplies: Users value speed and directness in frequent micro-interactions; design should favor instant reveals over choreographed motion
facade.rejectImplies: Users expect visual continuity and spatial awareness; even quick actions should have animated context to feel less jarring

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer persistent navigation over tabbed switching for quick context-switching between clients and projects
facade.label: Sidebar always visible
facade.acceptImplies: Build a collapsible-but-present sidebar as primary nav structure; information architecture should assume users jump between sections 5+ times per session
facade.rejectImplies: Explore tab-based or modal navigation; information can be deeper/narrower per view since users commit to a section before navigating away

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer visual, scannable client overviews (cards with photos, status badges) over dense contact tables for quick relationship context
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data vs key metrics — card-based summaries vs tabular detail
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values at-a-glance visual density and personality in client data; design should prioritize card layouts, avatars, and status indicators over spreadsheet-style tables
facade.rejectImplies: User needs high information density and structured filtering; tabular layouts with sortable columns and bulk actions may be more appropriate than visual card presentations

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers may prefer clean, low-distraction dashboards that reduce cognitive load during busy client work sessions
facade.label: Dark, minimal interface
facade.acceptImplies: Explore dark mode and sparse layouts with significant whitespace as core aesthetic directions
facade.rejectImplies: Freelancers may need visual richness or lighter themes; pursue brighter, more information-dense interface approaches instead

axes.label: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
Whether they favor dense information dashboards vs. progressive disclosure (showing only what's needed now)—reveals workflow preference for freelancers juggling multiple clients
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step

axes.label: Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a tool that feels like a business partner or a supportive coach
Whether they prefer calm, minimal atmosphere vs. energizing, motivational atmosphere—reveals emotional context they want while managing their business
Whether they want financial/metrics-first design vs. relationship/client-first design—reveals what information hierarchy matters most for their freelance priorities
Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered transitions—reveals if they value speed/efficiency or thoughtful, guided experiences

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want financial/metrics-first design vs. relationship/client-first design—reveals what information hierarchy matters most for their freelance priorities

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered transitions—reveals if they value speed/efficiency or thoughtful, guided experiences
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo
